## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 26, 1956, the incredibly massive, immensely bold, and historically explosive nationalization of the Suez Canal was officially and aggressively announced by Egyptian President Gamal Abdel Nasser. This massive geopolitical maneuver seized absolute control of the incredibly vital waterway from British and French corporate interests. The aggressive nationalization instantly triggered the massive, catastrophic Suez Crisis, leading to a disastrous, highly condemned military invasion by Britain, France, and Israel that fundamentally destroyed European imperial prestige in the Middle East.

On July 26, 1953, Fidel Castro, leading a massive, highly radicalized group of incredibly passionate Cuban revolutionaries, aggressively launched a massive, armed assault on the Moncada Barracks in Santiago de Cuba. While the incredibly fierce military attack was an absolute, disastrous tactical failure that resulted in Castro's massive imprisonment, the audacious raid fundamentally and aggressively ignited the massive Cuban Revolution, eventually leading to the absolute, total overthrow of the US-backed Batista dictatorship in 1959.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 26, 1999, the incredibly massive, brutally intense, and deeply strategic Kargil War was officially and aggressively declared a complete, absolute military victory for the Republic of India. Operation Vijay successfully concluded as the Indian Armed Forces violently and aggressively cleared the absolute final Pakistani infiltrators from the freezing Himalayan heights. This incredibly monumental date is fiercely and heavily celebrated annually across the entire nation as Kargil Vijay Diwas, honoring the absolute, uncompromising sacrifice of the Indian military.

On July 26, 1856, George Bernard Shaw, the incredibly massive, globally renowned, and highly influential Irish playwright and massive political activist, was officially born in Dublin. While not Indian, his incredibly massive, sharp intellectual critiques of British imperialism heavily and aggressively resonated with the massive Indian educated elite. His profound, massive works, heavily championing socialist ideals and absolute anti-colonialism, deeply influenced the intellectual architecture of massive Indian independence leaders, including Jawaharlal Nehru.
